current_role: Word-level reflexive-pronoun carrier with Jyutping and “self / oneself” note
decisive_difference: Does not preserve antecedent candidates, local versus long-distance binding, grammatical function, possession, perspective, or intensifier status.

current_role: Represents `我/你/佢` and plural forms as referring NPs
observed_overlap: A person-marked pronoun may precede `自己`.
decisive_difference: Lam treats complex `keuihjihgei` as locally bound, while Matthews and Yip present pronoun-plus-`jihgei` as an intensifier; ordinary reference alone preserves neither analysis.

current_role: Surface carrier for modifier or possessor plus nominal material
observed_overlap: Can contain `自己 + classifier/noun` or `自己嘅 + noun`.
decisive_difference: The reflexive possessor requires an antecedent relation and may permit more than one antecedent; ordinary possession does not encode binding.

current_role: Ordinary subject, object, oblique, or complement slot
observed_overlap: Bare `自己` can occupy an argument position.
decisive_difference: Argument position does not determine the antecedent, binding distance, or anaphoric/logophoric interpretation.

observed_overlap: Shares the same bare surface form.
decisive_difference: Lam explicitly excludes this use from the binding analysis; it does not itself establish an argument-antecedent relation.

observed_overlap: Shares bare `自己` and may lack a single overt antecedent.
decisive_difference: Chen et al. distinguish generic from anaphoric and intensifier uses; merging it would fabricate a binding relation.

current_role: Research question for contextually omitted arguments
observed_overlap: Both phenomena require discourse context and may lack a nearby overt NP.
decisive_difference: A reflexive is overt and requires interpretation of its reference; null arguments concern an unexpressed constituent.
